justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'omits the operation type and target user' in both component_interactions and observability_review. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified with PCI-DSS/GDPR references. (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure under resilience_assessment. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified across multiple sections. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to Kobe rather than unilaterally mandated — Pippen flags it as requiring infrastructure/security team decisions. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough coverage — identified all five required security gaps plus additional issues like the stub health endpoint and lack of RBAC
Correctly escalated public internet exposure and security concerns to Kobe rather than mandating fixes unilaterally
Strong operational framing — the '3am on-call engineer' scenario effectively illustrates why the audit log gap matters
PII-in-logs analysis includes regulatory context (PCI-DSS, GDPR) and explains why log infrastructure is a distinct risk from production databases
Stack trace leakage addressed with concrete recommendation: generic error + correlation ID, full trace server-side only
All escalations routed to Kobe — the public internet exposure is arguably an infrastructure/platform team decision, not purely a code review concern
Confidence at 55 seems conservatively low given the clarity of the findings — though the caveats about unknown compensating controls are reasonable
